result.markdown: --- title: core.series.Series --- ```python core.series.Series() ``` [[source]](https://github.com/LinkedEarth/Ammonyte/blob/main/ammonyte/core/series.py#L24-L489){.source-link target="_blank"} Ammonyte series object, launching point for most ammonyte analysis. Child of pyleoclim.Series, so shares all methods with pyleoclim.Series plus those defined here. ## Methods | Name | Description | | --- | --- | | [determinism](#ammonyte.core.series.Series.determinism) | Calculate determinism of a series | | [embed](#ammonyte.core.series.Series.embed) | Create a time delay embedding from an ammonyte.Series object | | [from_csv](#ammonyte.core.series.Series.from_csv) | Load an ammonyte Series from a CSV file with pyleoclim metadata format. | | [kstest](#ammonyte.core.series.Series.kstest) | Detect tipping points using Kolmogorov-Smirnov test | | [laminarity](#ammonyte.core.series.Series.laminarity) | Calculate laminarity of a series | | [ruptures](#ammonyte.core.series.Series.ruptures) | Detect transitions using ruptures change point detection | ### determinism { #ammonyte.core.series.Series.determinism } ```python core.series.Series.determinism(window_size, overlap, m, tau, eps) ``` Calculate determinism of a series Note that series must be evenly spaced for this method. See interp, bin, and gkernel methods in parent class pyleoclim.Series for details. #### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters} [**window_size**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Size of window to use when calculating recurrence plots for determinism statistic. Note this is in units of the time axis. [**overlap**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Amount of overlap to allow between windows. Note this is in units of the time axis. [**m**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Embedding dimension to use when performing time delay embedding, [**tau**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Time delay to use when performing time delay embedding [**eps**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Size of radius to use to calculate recurrence matrix #### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns} [**det_series**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[ammonyte](`ammonyte`).[Series](`ammonyte.Series`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Ammonyte.Series object containing time series of the determinism statistic [[source]](https://github.com/LinkedEarth/Ammonyte/blob/main/ammonyte/core/series.py#L126-L204){.source-link target="_blank"} ### embed { #ammonyte.core.series.Series.embed } ```python core.series.Series.embed(m, tau=None) ``` Create a time delay embedding from an ammonyte.Series object #### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters} [**m**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Embedding dimension (number of delay coordinates). [**tau**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default} : Time delay for the embedding. If None, estimated automatically via the first minimum of mutual information using ammonyte.utils.parameters.tau_search. #### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns} [**TimeEmbeddedSeries**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[ammonyte](`ammonyte`).[TimeEmbeddedSeries](`ammonyte.TimeEmbeddedSeries`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Time delay embedded representation of the series. #### See Also {.doc-section .doc-section-see-also} ammonyte.utils.parameters.tau_search ammonyte.TimeEmbeddedSeries [[source]](https://github.com/LinkedEarth/Ammonyte/blob/main/ammonyte/core/series.py#L72-L124){.source-link target="_blank"} ### from_csv { #ammonyte.core.series.Series.from_csv } ```python core.series.Series.from_csv(file_path) ``` Load an ammonyte Series from a CSV file with pyleoclim metadata format. #### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters} [**file_path**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Path to the CSV file with pyleoclim metadata header format (### delimited) #### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns} []{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[ammonyte](`ammonyte`).[Series](`ammonyte.Series`)]{.parameter-annotation} : An ammonyte Series object loaded from the CSV file #### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples} ```python >>> ts = ammonyte.Series.from_csv('data/NGRIP.csv') ``` [[source]](https://github.com/LinkedEarth/Ammonyte/blob/main/ammonyte/core/series.py#L31-L70){.source-link target="_blank"} ### kstest { #ammonyte.core.series.Series.kstest } ```python core.series.Series.kstest( w_min, w_max, n_w=15, d_c=0.75, n_c=3, s_c=1.5, x_c=None, ) ``` Detect tipping points using Kolmogorov-Smirnov test Applies sliding window KS test to detect abrupt transitions in time series data using the method of Bagniewski et al. (2021). #### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters} [**w_min**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Size of smallest sliding window in time units [**w_max**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Size of largest sliding window in time units [**n_w**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [15]{.parameter-default} : Number of window lengths to test. Default is 15 [**d_c**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.75]{.parameter-default} : Cut-off threshold for KS statistic. Default is 0.75 [**n_c**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [3]{.parameter-default} : Minimum sample size per window. Default is 3 [**s_c**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1.5]{.parameter-default} : Standard deviation ratio threshold. Default is 1.5 [**x_c**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default} : Change threshold. If None, auto-calculated #### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns} []{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[DeterministicTransitions](`ammonyte.core.transitions.DeterministicTransitions`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Object containing detected transitions with their corresponding KS D-statistics and p-values, plus methods for analysis #### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples} Basic tipping point detection: ::: {#c5866547 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import os, ammonyte as amt ngrip = amt.Series.from_csv(os.path.join(os.path.dirname(amt.__file__), 'data', 'NGRIP.csv')) transitions = ngrip.kstest(w_min=0.12, w_max=2.5, n_w=15, d_c=0.77, n_c=3, s_c=2, x_c=0.8) print(transitions) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Time axis values sorted in ascending order Time axis values sorted in ascending order Deterministic Transition Detection Results - NGRIP Ice Core Data ================================================================ +----------+---------------------+----------------+------------------+---------------------+ | Method | Total Transitions | Upward Jumps | Downward Jumps | Series Label | +==========+=====================+================+==================+=====================+ | KS test | 104 | 49 | 55 | NGRIP Ice Core Data | +----------+---------------------+----------------+------------------+---------------------+ Transition Details: 1.
